programme.desc: Rick takes us to his family home at Trevose Head on the north Cornish coast, where as a child he would go fishing with his father. He explores the wild yet beautiful landscape of the Land's End peninsula with Kurt Jackson.

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Deep in the Cornish countryside, Rick Stein meets an extraordinary family who make some of the best Gouda cheese in Britain, from which he cooks a tortilla dish with caramelised apples, onions and Cornish Gouda.

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick Stein meets young chef Tom Adams, who runs Combeshead Farm, a leading field-to-fork restaurant in Cornwall. Here they grow and produce all of their own food, from sourdough bread to pork pies, as well as rearing a small heard of Mangalitsa pigs.

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: The town of Launceston in east Cornwall is bypassed by many who visit the county, yet Rick discovers that it's home to an unusual superstition and a rare type of Norman castle. Rick meets two musicians who combine modern and traditional ways.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy tests the Spyker C8 Spyder, while Richard and James try their hand at minicabbing and road test two new MPVs - the Renault Scenic and the Ford C-Max. The Star in a Reasonably Priced Car is Lionel Richie of the Commodores.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy tests the new Ford GT and the Toyota Prius - an electric/petrol engine hybrid; Richard celebrates the Rover V8 engine, and James reviews the Maserati Quattroporte. The Star in a Reasonably Priced Car is Martin Clunes.

programme.title: Mortimer and Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul and Bob travel to the picturesque River Usk in Wales, where Paul's father first taught him to fish as a young child. Their quarry is the wild brown trout. They discuss their respective childhoods and reflect on their own family lives. In their cosy cottage accommodation in the heart of the countryside they compare school photographs and Paul serenades Bob with his guitar.

programme.title: Mediterranean With Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon continues his journey in Libya, a country well off the tourist trail and torn apart by revolution, insurgents from the so-called Islamic State and western air strikes. He then heads to Tunisia, a country that unlike its neighbour, has long been a tourist destination. Heading deep into the desert, the backdrop for scenes in the `Star Wars' films, Simon visits the fortress village of Chenini.

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Asian Adventure
programme.desc: In this episode they're in Hong Kong, where they discover the secrets of `wok chi' at one of the city's street food stalls, they cook with a grandma in her tiny kitchen, and the bikers are sent on their way with a Chinese good luck ceremony.

programme.desc: Rick journeys inland from the majestic Camel Estuary to one of Britain's finest vineyards, where he joins in harvesting the grapes to discover that the Cornish weather is perfect for making some of the best wine in the country.

programme.desc: Rick explores one of Cornwall's hidden secrets, the secluded and stunning Fowey Estuary, joining his friend on a boat trip to learn about the history of the area and getting a unique view of the beautiful buildings along the river bank.

programme.desc: In this episode they're in Hong Kong, where they discover the secrets of `wok chi' at one of the city's street food stalls, they cook with a grandma in her tiny kitchen, and the bikers are sent on their way with a Chinese good luck ceremony.

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick takes us to his family home at Trevose Head on the north Cornish coast, where as a child he would go fishing with his father. He explores the wild yet beautiful landscape of the Land's End peninsula with Kurt Jackson.

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Deep in the Cornish countryside, Rick Stein meets an extraordinary family who make some of the best Gouda cheese in Britain, from which he cooks a tortilla dish with caramelised apples, onions and Cornish Gouda.

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick Stein meets young chef Tom Adams, who runs Combeshead Farm, a leading field-to-fork restaurant in Cornwall. Here they grow and produce all of their own food, from sourdough bread to pork pies, as well as rearing a small heard of Mangalitsa pigs.

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: The town of Launceston in east Cornwall is bypassed by many who visit the county, yet Rick discovers that it's home to an unusual superstition and a rare type of Norman castle. Rick meets two musicians who combine modern and traditional ways.

programme.title: Mortimer and Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul and Bob travel to the picturesque River Usk in Wales, where Paul's father first taught him to fish as a young child. Their quarry is the wild brown trout. They discuss their respective childhoods and reflect on their own family lives. In their cosy cottage accommodation in the heart of the countryside they compare school photographs and Paul serenades Bob with his guitar.

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: James reveals the cars that turned post-war Germany and Japan into motoring powerhouses at the expense of Britain and the US. On his travels he encounters classic E-Types, Mustangs and the German and Japanese upstarts. He also has an unfortunate encounter with an Austin Allegro - the car that helped destroy the British car industry.

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: James goes off-road to tell the story of how the 4x4 conquered the world. From the wartime jeep and the global struggle between Land Rover and Land Cruiser, to the white-knuckle world of rallying and boy racers. James conquers Mount Fuji and races through the sun scorched Mojave Desert to decide which is the ultimate people's 4x4.

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: James explores the wilder shores of motoring to discover what happened to the cars of the future that we were promised. From improbable steam cars and ludicrous jet turbines he reveals how the petrol engine and the power it gave us came to dominate the 20th century. He'll take to the waves in an amphibious car, risk his life at the wheel of a notorious electric scooter and take a hair raising trip in the fastest driverless car on earth.

programme.title: Hornby A Model World
programme.desc: A perfect diecast replica of a Merchant Navy steam locomotive is the task for Hornby's design team. Engineer Chris tries to capture the authentic sounds of a Light Pacific loco.

programme.title: Hornby A Model World
programme.desc: Experienced designer Carl has a challenge on his hands attempting to recreate a WWI biplane as a 1/48th scale Corgi model.

programme.title: Train Rescue Down Under
programme.desc: In Perth, the pressure is on for a team tackling a high-stakes engine rebuild, and in Townsville, a crew of experts build a massive underframe for a freight powerhouse.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: In a dramatic start to the new series, the West Midlands Ambulance Service treat a man who stabbed a police dog, a woman with a suspected stroke and a man on his fourth bout of pneumonia.

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Hard-working ambulance crews help a young woman who fell down a flight of stairs and a shivering pensioner with possible sepsis, as well as a sparky 95-year-old who's had a fall.

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: On shift with West Midlands Ambulance Service who treat a young lad with a fever, a golf-loving pensioner with a heart problem and a woman convinced she has lung cancer.

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: An asthmatic deteriorates rapidly in the ambulance, and the crews are called to a 30-year-old car that's been driven into a wall by a 90-year-old driver.

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: James reveals the cars that turned post-war Germany and Japan into motoring powerhouses at the expense of Britain and the US. On his travels he encounters classic E-Types, Mustangs and the German and Japanese upstarts. He also has an unfortunate encounter with an Austin Allegro - the car that helped destroy the British car industry.

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: James goes off-road to tell the story of how the 4x4 conquered the world. From the wartime jeep and the global struggle between Land Rover and Land Cruiser, to the white-knuckle world of rallying and boy racers. James conquers Mount Fuji and races through the sun scorched Mojave Desert to decide which is the ultimate people's 4x4.

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: James explores the wilder shores of motoring to discover what happened to the cars of the future that we were promised. From improbable steam cars and ludicrous jet turbines he reveals how the petrol engine and the power it gave us came to dominate the 20th century. He'll take to the waves in an amphibious car, risk his life at the wheel of a notorious electric scooter and take a hair raising trip in the fastest driverless car on earth.

programme.title: Hornby A Model World
programme.desc: A perfect diecast replica of a Merchant Navy steam locomotive is the task for Hornby's design team. Engineer Chris tries to capture the authentic sounds of a Light Pacific loco.

programme.title: Hornby A Model World
programme.desc: Experienced designer Carl has a challenge on his hands attempting to recreate a WWI biplane as a 1/48th scale Corgi model.

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: Boycie and Marlene drive Tyler back to his old university where his band, `Puddle of Agony', is about to perform its very first gig. The staff, left alone to look after the Grange, decide to help a couple of ghost-hunters who have arrived in the area seeking phantoms and ghouls. While Tyler's career seems to be slipping downhill, Bryan, Jed and Mrs Cakeworthy appear to be in seventh heaven.

programme.title: Red Dwarf
programme.desc: Lister decides to read Rimmer's diary and discovers a leak from the `Stasis' (suspended-animation chamber) that creates a doorway to the past. Passing through the leak, the crew are able to go back in time to a point before the rest of the crew were wiped out and might just be able to bring someone back with them.

programme.title: Harry Enfield and Chums
programme.desc: Look out for the likes of `The Scousers, The Slobs and The Double Take Brothers'.

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lucy is stressed, as she has to make an important speech at a recruitment conference and has nobody to help her write it. Sensing an opportunity to impress her, Lee offers his services, which upsets Tim, who also thinks he is the best man for the job. What started off as a small job soon turns into a competition between Tim and Lee.

programme.title: Men Behaving Badly
programme.desc: Dermot is trying to impress Deborah by offering her his body. Gary is trying to impress her by installing a burglar alarm. Which is the better offer?

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Agnes is upset that her son Trevor has been called back to the missions in Africa, but is determined to give him a massive send-off to remind him what a loving family he has back home in Ireland. Meanwhile, daughter Cathy's family counselling session descends into chaos, and Buster and Dermot run into trouble with a circus lion. The only person having a good day is neighbour Winnie.

programme.title: Hi-De-Hi!
programme.desc: Ted challenges the Marines to race on their assault course.

programme.title: Terry and June
programme.desc: Terry wins a giant jigsaw puzzle in his firm's raffle, but completing it proves to be anything but relaxing.

programme.title: Terry and June
programme.desc: Deciding to sell his old snooker table, Terry places an advertisement in the local newspaper. Confusion over telephone numbers sends Terry and June on a fortune-hunting trail.

programme.title: Terry and June
programme.desc: Terry is none too happy when June takes a temporary job (shocking in the early eighties!) - and when their professional paths cross, life becomes very complicated.

programme.title: Terry and June
programme.desc: June tries to learn to play the cello. How's Terry going to cope with all the practice? Ear-muffs, maybe?

programme.title: Terry and June
programme.desc: Much loved sitcom following gaffe-prone Terry and his loving wife June as they enjoy suburban life in '80s Surrey.

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance

programme.desc: Once-great triumphs of marine engineering now left to rust include a ship graveyard in Maryland colonised by wildlife, and a Cold War sub.

programme.title: Speed with Guy Martin

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave heads to Bolton to pick up a classic Italian sports car - a Lancia Fulvia 1.3 Sport. This classic car was styled by the well known Milanese coach builder, Zagato. Zagato are famous for collaborating with high end car brands.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek rubs shoulders with pop music royalty when he picks up a classic Morgan sports car. A World War Two ambulance arrives at Thornton le Dale and a Ford Model T needs a new home.

programme.title: World at War
programme.desc: While the full force of the Nazi war machine sweeps across Europe, an uneasy calm settles on Britain as Churchill replaces Chamberlain as prime minister.

programme.title: Secret Nazi Bases
programme.sub-title: Dag Bromberg: A City in the Forest
programme.desc: As the Red Army approached a forest in Poland toward the end of the war, they stumbled upon a complex containing more than 1,000 structures across 25sqkm.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team of experts visit Beverley Minster in Yorkshire. Pieces under scrutiny include a valuable medieval ring dug up on a farm; two Victorian paintings given in exchange for a gambling debt; and a car rescued from a pig farm's outbuilding, which turns out to be a former rally winner once driven by Stirling Moss.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team are back for a second visit to Beverley Minster in the East Riding of Yorkshire. Hundreds of visitors pack the nave eager to see the experts. Amongst the objects caught on camera are a pair of valuable medical leech jars once used to bleed patients and a curious sideboard that hides secret drawers that took the owners 30 years to find.

programme.title: World at War
programme.desc: Despite having the largest pre-war European army, France's outdated forces were humiliated by the Germans. Identifying a weakness in the Maginot Line, a defensive border which ran along the north east of France but did not extend to the channel in the west, German air and armour power simply skirted the line in the north and forged south.

programme.title: Secret Nazi Bases
programme.desc: A secret weapons programme in the heart of Austria that could have ended badly for the Allies - and may have indicated nuclear plans being concocted by Hitler.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team pore over more prized possessions. The experts are intrigued by a rare glass vase, items from Rudolf Hess's plane and Michael Jackson's hat.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: Asia is the largest and most extreme continent on our planet, stretching from the Arctic Circle in the north to the tropical forests on the equator. The animals here face the hottest deserts, tallest jungles and highest mountains found anywhere on Earth. But the continent has not always looked like this. These extreme worlds were created when India collided with the rest of Asia 30 million years ago, shaping the continent as we know it today.

programme.title: Ab Tumhare Hawale Watan Saathiyo
programme.desc: Kunal is in love with Shweta, who's waiting for her husband Rajeev to return from war. Kunal is torn between his love for Shweta and his duty as a soldier.

programme.title: Aatma - Feel it Around You
programme.desc: A single mother fights against the supernatural in order to hold on to her daughter.

programme.title: Behen Hogi Teri
programme.desc: Gattu is head over heels in love with his neighbour, Binny. However, he becomes disheartened when her family and the entire neighbourhood treat him as her brother.

programme.title: Samadhi
programme.desc: Lakhan Singh gives up his life of a dacoit to marry his love Champa. When Champa gets sick with cancer, the financial crunches force Lakhan to go back to his former criminal lifestyle.

programme.title: Ek Rishtaa: The Bond of Love
programme.desc: A large family encounter a crisis when disputes take place between the father and the son..

programme.title: Little John
programme.desc: John wants to make himself invisible in order to impress his girlfriend`s parents. Things go wrong and he is transformed into a thumb sized person.

programme.title: Taal
programme.desc: A man hopes that it's not too late to win the woman of his dreams after his family resists their romance.

programme.title: Koi ... Mil Gaya
programme.desc: Indian scientist Sanjay Mehra is convinced life exists beyond the earth and manages to send a message into space. When at last he receives an answer, no one believes there may be aliens out there but Sanjay and his pregnant wife, Sonia, see a UFO at first hand. Following a car accident, Sanjay dies and Sonia has their son, Rohit Mehra but as the boy grows physically, his mind remains that of a child.

programme.title: Saheb Biwi Aur Gangster
programme.desc: After the death of his first wife, a wealthy man re-marries another woman who subsequently becomes mentally unstable.

programme.title: Bamfaad
programme.desc: Two people meet accidentally and fall in love. They make brave choices and go through a path of love and loss to be with each other.

programme.title: Phool Bane Angarey
programme.desc: Namrata pledges to avenge her husband Ranjit`s death after he is killed by the powerful Bishamber.

programme.title: Main Tulsi Tere Aangan Ki
programme.desc: A man falls in love with a prostitute but marries the woman his mother has chosen for him instead.

programme.title: F.A.L.T.U
programme.desc: The story of a group of friends who don't get into a college and decide to start up their own University.

programme.title: Super Singh
programme.desc: A man discovers the true meaning of courage when he acquires superpowers.

programme.title: Article 15
programme.desc: Based on the socio-political situation of India, the lives of police officers are followed. In a country where discrimination is everywhere, whether related to race or caste, the officers embark on a journey of acceptance and change.

programme.title: Mom
programme.desc: God could not be everywhere, so he created mothers. What will a mother do in such a situation. Will she fight for her daughter's love knowing the consequences she may have to face?

programme.title: Socha Na Tha
programme.desc: Mr. Oberoi wants his younger son to settle down with a wife, but Viren dreams of true love, and sets out to find the girl of his dreams.

programme.title: Ganga Mai Ki Betiyaan
programme.desc: Ganga Mai is a resilient woman who is deserted by her husband for not giving birth to a son. Refusing to bow to societal expectations, she devotes herself to raising her three daughters with unwavering strength, grace, and affection.

programme.title: Saru
programme.desc: A kabaddi champion from Rajasthan moves to Mumbai, where she battles all odds, reunites with her family, and fights to get her mother her rightful place in the family while chasing her dreams.

programme.desc: Lakshmi is married to Shriniwas Dalvi, who belongs to a traditional joint family. While she strives to navigate challenges, she supports her husband's dreams.
